what.question: What is InvoratecAI?
what.answer: InvoratecAI is an AI-powered BIM platform that combines a Revit add-in (BIM AI) with a cloud-based collaboration platform (Web AI). It helps architecture, engineering, and construction teams work more efficiently through intelligent automation and real-time collaboration.

integration.question: Does it integrate with existing tools?
integration.answer: Yes, InvoratecAI integrates seamlessly with Autodesk Revit (2019+), and our Web AI platform works in any modern browser. We also support connections to project management tools, cloud storage, and other BIM software through our API.

training.question: How long does it take to learn?
training.answer: Most users become productive within a few hours thanks to our intuitive interface and natural language commands. We provide comprehensive onboarding, video tutorials, and live training sessions to ensure your team succeeds.

pricing.question: How is InvoratecAI priced?
pricing.answer: We offer flexible pricing plans based on team size and features needed. Contact us for a custom quote that fits your organization's requirements. Enterprise plans include dedicated support and custom integrations.

support.question: What support is available?
support.answer: All plans include access to our documentation and community forum. Professional and Enterprise plans include email support, live chat, and dedicated account management with priority response times.

content.intro: At InvoratecAI, we take the security of your data seriously. This Security Policy outlines the measures we implement to protect your information across our BIM AI (Revit Add-in) and Web AI (Cloud Platform) products.

subsection1.title: 1.1 Cloud Infrastructure
subsection1.intro: InvoratecAI is hosted on Google Cloud Platform (GCP), leveraging enterprise-grade security features:
items: Data Centers: Google's world-class data centers with SOC 1, SOC 2, and SOC 3 certifications
Network Security: Google Cloud's global network with built-in DDoS protection
Cloud Functions: Serverless architecture with automatic scaling and isolation
Firebase/Firestore: Real-time database with enterprise security and encryption

subsection2.title: 1.2 Data Storage
subsection2.intro: Your BIM models, project data, and user information are stored with multiple layers of protection:
items: Encryption at Rest: All data encrypted using AES-256 encryption
Encryption in Transit: TLS 1.3 for all data transmission
Isolated Storage: Company data segregated by organization ID
Backup & Recovery: Automated daily backups with point-in-time recovery

subsection1.title: 2.1 Authentication & Access Control
items: Password Security: SHA-256 password hashing with secure key derivation
Session Management: Secure session handling with automatic timeout
Role-Based Access: Admin, Manager, and User role permissions
User Approval Workflow: New users require admin approval before access

subsection2.title: 2.2 Revit Add-in Security
subsection2.intro: Our BIM AI Revit plugin implements additional security measures:
items: Code Signing: Digitally signed installer and DLL files
Local Data Protection: Encrypted local cache with secure storage
API Communication: All API calls use HTTPS with certificate pinning
Offline Security: Credentials never stored in plain text

subsection3.title: 2.3 Web Platform Security
items: CORS Protection: Strict cross-origin resource sharing policies
XSS Prevention: Content Security Policy headers and input sanitization
CSRF Protection: Token-based request validation
Secure Headers: HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options

subsection1.title: 3.1 AI Model Security
subsection1.intro: Our AI features (powered by Claude/Anthropic) follow strict data handling practices:
items: No Training on Your Data: Your project data is never used to train AI models
Ephemeral Processing: AI queries are processed in real-time and not retained
Prompt Filtering: Input validation to prevent prompt injection attacks
Response Sanitization: AI outputs are validated before display

subsection2.title: 3.2 Third-Party Integrations
items: Autodesk Integration: OAuth 2.0 with scoped permissions for ACC access
Email Services: TLS-encrypted email transmission via Gmail SMTP
API Keys: Securely stored in environment variables, never exposed to clients

subsection1.title: 4.1 Monitoring & Logging
items: Access Logs: All API access logged with timestamps and user identification
Error Tracking: Real-time error monitoring and alerting
Performance Monitoring: Cloud Function metrics and latency tracking
Audit Trail: Activity logs for user actions and data modifications

subsection2.title: 4.2 Incident Response
subsection2.intro: We maintain a comprehensive incident response plan:
items: Detection: Automated alerts for suspicious activity
Response: Defined escalation procedures and response team
Communication: Customer notification within 72 hours of confirmed breach
Recovery: Documented recovery procedures and post-incident review

section5.title: 5. Compliance & Certifications
items: GDPR: Compliant with European data protection requirements
CCPA: Compliant with California Consumer Privacy Act
Cloud Provider Certifications: Inherits GCP's SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and ISO 27017 certifications

section6.title: 6. Security Best Practices for Users
section6.intro: We recommend the following security practices:
items: Use strong, unique passwords (minimum 8 characters with mixed case and numbers)
Keep your Revit add-in updated to the latest version
Review user access permissions regularly
Report suspicious activity to our security team immediately
Use the web platform over HTTPS only

section7.title: 7. Security Contact
section7.intro: For security concerns or to report vulnerabilities, please contact us:

content.intro: This Privacy Policy describes how Invoratec Inc. ("InvoratecAI," "we," "us," or "our") collects, uses, and shares information about you when you use our BIM AI (Revit Add-in), Web AI (Cloud Platform), and related services (collectively, the "Services").

items: Account Information: Name, email address, company name, password, and role when you create an account
Project Data: BIM models, drawings, project information, tasks, and collaboration content you upload or create
Communications: Messages you send to us, including support requests and feedback
Payment Information: Billing details processed through our payment providers (we do not store full payment card numbers)

subsection2.title: 1.2 Information Collected Automatically

Device Information: Operating system, Revit version, browser type, and device identifiers

items: Provide Services: Operate and maintain the BIM AI and Web AI platforms
Process AI Queries: Power AI assistant features for BIM model analysis and design assistance
Enable Collaboration: Facilitate team collaboration, task management, and model sharing
Improve Services: Analyze usage patterns to enhance features and user experience
Communicate: Send service updates, security alerts, and support messages
Ensure Security: Detect and prevent fraud, abuse, and security incidents
Comply with Law: Meet legal obligations and respond to lawful requests

subsection1.title: 3.1 Service Providers
subsection1.intro: We share information with third-party service providers who assist in operating our Services:
items: Google Cloud Platform: Cloud infrastructure and data storage
Anthropic: AI processing for intelligent features
Email Services: Transactional email delivery
Analytics Providers: Usage analytics and performance monitoring

subsection2.title: 3.2 Within Your Organization

Team members can see shared projects and collaboration content
Usage reports may be available to organization administrators

subsection3.title: 3.3 Legal Requirements
subsection3.content: We may disclose information if required by law or in response to valid legal process.

subsection4.title: 3.4 Business Transfers
subsection4.content: In the event of a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, your information may be transferred as part of that transaction.

section4.title: 4. Data Retention
section4.intro: We retain your information for as long as necessary to:
items: Provide the Services you have requested
Comply with legal obligations
Resolve disputes and enforce agreements
Maintain business records for audit purposes
section4.note: When you delete your account, we will delete or anonymize your personal information within 90 days, except where retention is required by law.

subsection1.title: 5.1 Access and Portability
subsection1.content: You can access your data through the InvoratecAI platform and export your projects and information.

subsection2.title: 5.2 Correction

subsection3.title: 5.3 Deletion

subsection5.content: If you are in the European Economic Area (EEA) or California, you have additional rights under GDPR or CCPA respectively, including the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.

section6.intro: Your information may be transferred to and processed in countries other than your country of residence, including the United States. We implement appropriate safeguards for such transfers, including:

section7.content: Our Services are not intended for children under 16 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 16. If we learn that we have collected personal information from a child under 16, we will delete that information.

section8.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of material changes by posting the new policy on this page and updating the "Last Updated" date. We encourage you to review this policy periodically.

content.intro: This Cookie Policy explains how Invoratec Inc. ("InvoratecAI," "we," "us," or "our") uses cookies and similar technologies on our websites and applications. By using our Services, you consent to the use of cookies as described in this policy.

section1.title: 1. What Are Cookies?
section1.content: Cookies are small text files that are stored on your device (computer, tablet, or mobile) when you visit a website. They help websites remember your preferences and improve your browsing experience.
items: Local Storage: Stores data in your browser for longer periods
Session Storage: Stores data temporarily during a browsing session
Pixels/Web Beacons: Small images that track page visits and actions

subsection1.title: 2.1 Essential Cookies
subsection1.intro: These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be disabled. They include:

subsection2.title: 2.2 Functional Cookies
subsection2.intro: These cookies enable enhanced functionality and personalization:

subsection3.title: 2.3 Analytics Cookies
subsection3.intro: We use analytics cookies to understand how visitors use our website:
items: Google Analytics: Helps us understand website traffic and usage patterns
These cookies collect anonymous data about page views, time spent, and navigation

subsection4.title: 2.4 Marketing Cookies
subsection4.content: We currently do not use marketing or advertising cookies.

subsection1.title: 3.1 Browser Settings
subsection1.content: You can control cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers allow you to:
items: See what cookies are stored and delete them
Block third-party cookies
Block all cookies from specific sites
Block all cookies entirely
subsection1.note: Note: Blocking essential cookies may prevent the website from functioning properly.

subsection2.title: 3.2 Our Cookie Preferences
subsection2.content: You can manage your cookie preferences using our cookie consent banner when you first visit our site, or contact us to update your preferences.

section4.title: 4. Third-Party Cookies
section4.content: Some features on our site may set cookies from third parties:
items: Google Analytics (analytics)
Autodesk (for ACC integration features)
YouTube (if embedded videos are present)
section4.note: These third parties have their own privacy policies governing cookie use.

section5.title: 5. Cookie Duration
items: Session Cookies: Deleted when you close your browser
Persistent Cookies: Remain until they expire or you delete them

section6.title: 6. Updates to This Policy
section6.content: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated revision date.

content.intro: This Data Processing Agreement ("DPA") forms part of the Terms of Service between Invoratec Inc. ("InvoratecAI," "Processor," "we," or "us") and the customer ("Controller," "you"). This DPA sets forth the terms and conditions under which we will process Personal Data on your behalf.

section1.title: 1. Definitions
items: "Data Protection Laws" means GDPR, CCPA, and other applicable data protection legislation
"Personal Data" means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person
"Processing" means any operation performed on Personal Data
"Data Subject" means the individual to whom Personal Data relates
"Sub-processor" means any third party engaged by us to process Personal Data
"Security Incident" means any unauthorized access, acquisition, or disclosure of Personal Data

subsection1.title: 2.1 Controller and Processor
subsection1.intro: For the purposes of this DPA:
items: You (the customer) are the Controller of Personal Data submitted to the Services
InvoratecAI is the Processor acting on your behalf

subsection2.title: 2.2 Subject Matter
subsection2.intro: This DPA applies to the processing of Personal Data in connection with:
items: BIM AI (Revit Add-in) services
Web AI (Cloud Platform) services
Related support and consulting services

subsection1.title: 3.1 Categories of Data Subjects
items: Customer employees and contractors
Project stakeholders and collaborators
End users of your BIM projects

subsection2.title: 3.2 Types of Personal Data
items: Identity data: Names, usernames, job titles
Contact data: Email addresses, phone numbers
Professional data: Company affiliation, role, department
Technical data: IP addresses, device information, usage logs
Project data: Task assignments, comments, collaboration history

subsection3.title: 3.3 Processing Activities
items: User account management and authentication
Project data storage and synchronization
Collaboration and communication features
AI-powered analysis and assistance
Analytics and service improvement
Technical support and troubleshooting

subsection1.title: 4.1 Processing Instructions
items: Process Personal Data only on your documented instructions
Inform you if we believe an instruction violates Data Protection Laws
Not process Personal Data for any purpose other than providing the Services

subsection2.title: 4.2 Confidentiality
items: Ensure personnel processing Personal Data are bound by confidentiality obligations
Limit access to Personal Data to those who need it to perform their duties

subsection3.title: 4.3 Security Measures
subsection3.intro: Implement appropriate technical and organizational measures including:
items: Encryption of Personal Data at rest and in transit
Access controls and authentication mechanisms
Regular security assessments and penetration testing
Incident response and disaster recovery procedures
Employee security training and awareness

subsection1.title: 5.1 Authorization
subsection1.content: You authorize us to engage Sub-processors to process Personal Data. A list of current Sub-processors is available at /terms/subprocessors.

subsection2.title: 5.2 Sub-processor Requirements
items: Enter into written agreements with Sub-processors imposing equivalent data protection obligations
Remain liable for Sub-processor compliance
Notify you of new Sub-processors with at least 30 days' notice
Allow you to object to new Sub-processors

section6.title: 6. Data Subject Rights
section6.intro: We will assist you in responding to Data Subject requests including:
items: Right of access
Right to rectification
Right to erasure ("right to be forgotten")
Right to data portability
Right to restriction of processing
Right to object

subsection1.title: 7.1 Notification

subsection2.title: 7.2 Incident Details

Categories and approximate number of Data Subjects affected

subsection3.title: 7.3 Cooperation

section8.title: 8. International Data Transfers
section8.intro: Personal Data may be transferred to countries outside the EEA. We ensure appropriate safeguards through:
items: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission
Transfers to countries with adequate data protection (as determined by the EC)
Other valid transfer mechanisms under Data Protection Laws

section9.title: 9. Audit Rights

content.intro: These AI Additional Terms ("AI Terms") supplement and are incorporated into the InvoratecAI Terms of Service. They govern your use of AI-powered features within our BIM AI and Web AI platforms. By using our AI features, you agree to these additional terms.

subsection1.title: 1.1 BIM AI Features
subsection1.intro: Our BIM AI platform provides intelligent assistance for Revit and construction workflows:
items: Natural Language Queries: Ask questions about your BIM models in plain language
Element Analysis: AI-powered analysis of model elements and properties
Smart Suggestions: Intelligent recommendations for model improvements
Documentation Assistance: AI-generated descriptions and reports
Clash Detection Insights: AI analysis of coordination issues

subsection2.title: 1.2 Web AI Features
subsection2.intro: Our Web AI platform enables intelligent web content interaction:
items: Content Summarization: AI-generated summaries of web pages
Question Answering: Get answers based on web content
Research Assistance: AI-powered research and information gathering
Translation Support: Multi-language content understanding

subsection1.title: 2.1 AI Provider
subsection1.content: InvoratecAI utilizes Anthropic's Claude AI models to power our intelligent features. Anthropic is a leading AI safety company committed to building reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

subsection2.title: 2.2 Processing Architecture
items: AI queries are processed in real-time through secure API connections
Your data is transmitted securely using industry-standard encryption
AI processing occurs in Anthropic's secure infrastructure
Results are returned directly to you without intermediate storage

subsection1.title: 3.1 What Data is Processed
subsection1.intro: When you use AI features, the following data may be processed:
items: Query Content: Your questions, prompts, and instructions
Context Data: Relevant portions of BIM models or web content needed to answer your query
Conversation History: Previous messages in the current session for context continuity

subsection2.title: 3.2 Data NOT Used for Training
items: Your data is NOT used to train AI models
Your queries are NOT stored by Anthropic after processing
Your BIM models and project data remain confidential
AI interactions are processed ephemerally

subsection3.title: 3.3 Data Retention
items: Processing: Queries are processed in real-time and not persisted by the AI provider
Logging: We may log metadata (timestamps, token counts) for billing and quality purposes
History: Conversation history within a session is temporary and cleared when you start a new session

subsection1.title: 4.1 Permitted Uses
items: Querying and analyzing your own BIM models and data
Generating documentation and reports for your projects
Asking questions about construction and design best practices
Using AI suggestions to improve your workflows

subsection2.title: 4.2 Prohibited Uses
subsection2.intro: You agree NOT to use AI features to:

shanghai-xintiandi.title: Shanghai Xintiandi Taiping Bridge Block 132 Smart BIM Platform
shanghai-xintiandi.summary: A BIM-based smart management platform achieving full lifecycle digital management for a complex mixed-use development, reducing coordination time by 40%
shanghai-xintiandi.challenge: Shanghai Xintiandi Taiping Bridge Block 132 is a mega mixed-use complex comprising office, retail, and hotel components. The project faced challenges including complex multi-discipline coordination, inefficient information sharing, and difficulty in real-time construction progress monitoring. Traditional project management approaches could not meet the refined management requirements of such a complex development.
shanghai-xintiandi.solution: We deployed a BIM-based smart management platform integrating 3D model viewing, task management, progress tracking, and multi-discipline coordination. The platform enables digital management across design, construction, and operations phases, with an AI assistant supporting natural language queries and intelligent analytics.
shanghai-xintiandi.results: Project coordination time reduced by 40%, information sharing efficiency improved by 60%, and construction rework rate decreased by 35%. The platform successfully supported collaborative work among 20+ professional teams, achieving on-time project delivery.

ordos-smart-scenic.title: Ordos Smart Scenic Area Platform
ordos-smart-scenic.summary: Empowering cultural tourism with refined operations through AI and BIM technology for intelligent scenic area management and visitor service optimization
ordos-smart-scenic.challenge: As an emerging tourist destination, Ordos needed a smart management platform covering multiple scenic areas. Challenges included: widely distributed attractions, complex facility management, fluctuating visitor traffic, and scattered operational data. Traditional management models struggled to achieve refined operations and real-time decision-making.
ordos-smart-scenic.solution: We built an integrated smart scenic area platform combining BIM models, facility management, visitor services, and data analytics. 3D visualization displays the complete scenic area landscape, an AI assistant supports rapid queries and analysis for operations staff, and real-time monitoring tracks visitor flow and facility status.
ordos-smart-scenic.results: Scenic area operational efficiency improved by 35%, facility maintenance costs reduced by 20%, and visitor satisfaction significantly increased. The platform supports unified management across multiple scenic areas, providing a successful model for digital transformation in cultural tourism.

daxing-airport.title: Daxing Airport Integrated O&M Platform
daxing-airport.summary: Intelligent operations and maintenance management for a mega airport facility, achieving full lifecycle equipment management and multi-system coordination
daxing-airport.challenge: Beijing Daxing International Airport, as the world's largest single-building terminal, features an enormous variety of equipment and complex systems with extremely challenging O&M requirements. An integrated operations platform was needed to support massive equipment management, multi-system coordination, and intelligent decision-making.
daxing-airport.solution: We built a BIM-based integrated O&M platform integrating MEP, HVAC, fire protection, and other multi-discipline systems. The platform delivers equipment asset management, inspection and maintenance, energy monitoring, and emergency response capabilities, with an AI assistant supporting O&M staff in rapid problem identification and solution retrieval.
daxing-airport.results: Equipment fault response time reduced by 45%, O&M costs decreased by 30%, and energy consumption optimized by 15%. The platform successfully manages over 100,000 pieces of equipment and supports thousands of O&M personnel in daily operations, becoming a benchmark project for smart airport construction.

configuration.title: Configuration Guide
configuration.intro: Learn how to configure InvoratecAI to match your workflow.
configuration.accountDesc: Manage your profile, subscription, and notification preferences in the account settings panel.
configuration.projectDesc: Configure project-specific settings including team members, milestones, and budget tracking.
configuration.accDesc: Connect your Autodesk Construction Cloud account to sync projects, models, and coordination data.

api.title: API Reference
api.intro: InvoratecAI provides a REST API for integration with your existing tools and workflows.
api.authDesc: All API requests require authentication using an API key. Generate your API key in the account settings.
api.endpointsDesc: Our API includes endpoints for projects, tasks, users, and AI queries.
api.rateLimitDesc: API requests are limited to 100 requests per minute per API key. Enterprise plans include higher limits.

webTasks.overviewDesc: The Web Platform's task management system is built on a flexible database architecture supporting 45+ property types. Create unlimited custom pages with any combination of properties to match your project needs.
webTasks.creatingPagesDesc: Task pages are containers for organizing your work. Each page can have its own set of custom properties.
webTasks.creatingPagesStep1: Click the + button in the sidebar to create a new page
webTasks.creatingPagesStep2: Name your page and choose a template or start blank
webTasks.creatingPagesStep3: Add properties by clicking the + column header
webTasks.propertiesDesc: InvoratecAI supports a wide variety of property types to capture any kind of project data:
webTasks.filteringDesc: Filter your tasks by any property using the filter bar. Combine multiple filters with AND/OR logic. Save filter views for quick access to commonly used task lists.
webTasks.excelImportDesc: Bulk import tasks from Excel spreadsheets or export your data for reporting:
webTasks.excelStep1: Click the Import button and select your Excel file
webTasks.excelStep2: Map columns to properties in the import dialog

webViewer.overviewDesc: The 3D Model Viewer is powered by Autodesk Platform Services (APS) and supports viewing IFC, NWD, RVT, and other BIM formats directly in your browser without installing any software.
webViewer.navigationDesc: Use these controls to navigate your 3D model:
webViewer.navOrbit: Left-click + drag: Orbit around the model
webViewer.selectionDesc: Click on any element to select it. The property panel will show all parameters and properties for the selected element. Multi-select using Ctrl+click.
webViewer.isolationDesc: Right-click on selected elements and choose 'Isolate' to hide all other elements. This helps you focus on specific areas of your model. Use 'Show All' to restore visibility.
webViewer.sectionsDesc: Create section cuts through your model using the section plane tools. Adjust the position and orientation of section planes to expose interior details.
webViewer.measurementsDesc: Measure distances, areas, and angles directly in the 3D view. Click the measurement tool, then click two or more points to measure.
webViewer.propertiesDesc: The property panel displays all BIM properties for selected elements including type parameters, instance parameters, and custom properties.

webTimeline.overviewDesc: The Timeline & Gantt view provides visual project scheduling with task dependencies, milestones, and progress tracking. Plan your project timeline and monitor progress in real-time.
webTimeline.ganttViewDesc: The Gantt chart displays tasks as horizontal bars on a timeline. The length of each bar represents the task duration. Tasks are color-coded by status.
webTimeline.schedStep1: Drag task bars horizontally to change start dates
webTimeline.schedStep2: Drag the edges of task bars to adjust duration
webTimeline.schedStep3: Set dates directly in the task properties panel
webTimeline.dependenciesDesc: Link tasks together to create dependencies. When a predecessor task moves, dependent tasks automatically adjust. Use the Relation property to link tasks together.
webTimeline.milestonesDesc: Mark important project dates as milestones. Milestones appear as diamond shapes on the timeline and can be linked to tasks and deliverables.
webTimeline.progressDesc: Track completion percentage for each task. The Gantt chart shows progress as a filled portion of the task bar, making it easy to see which tasks are on track.

webCoordination.overviewDesc: The Coordination module helps you manage multi-discipline clash detection and resolution. Sync clashes from Autodesk Construction Cloud and track resolution status.
webCoordination.accSyncDesc: Connect to your ACC project to import coordination clashes:
webCoordination.accStep2: Click 'Sync from ACC' and select your clash test
webCoordination.accStep3: Clashes are imported with their 3D viewpoints
webCoordination.clashReviewDesc: Each clash includes a 3D viewpoint that zooms to the conflict location. Review clashes in context with the full model visible.
webCoordination.issueTrackingDesc: Assign clashes to team members, set priority and due dates, and track resolution status. Add comments and attachments to document the resolution process.
webCoordination.disciplinesDesc: Tag clashes by discipline (MEP, Structural, Architectural) for filtering and reporting. Generate reports showing clash counts by discipline and status.

webSheets.overviewDesc: The Shop Drawings module provides 2D drawing viewing with markup tools and ACC Build integration. Review, annotate, and approve construction documents.
webSheets.viewingDesc: Upload PDF or DWG files to view in the browser. Navigate between pages, zoom, pan, and search for text within documents.
webSheets.markupDesc: Annotate drawings with these markup tools:
webSheets.markupTool1: Pen and highlighter for freehand drawing
webSheets.markupTool2: Shapes including rectangles, circles, and arrows
webSheets.accBuildDesc: Sync markups with ACC Build to share annotations with the field team. Markups created in InvoratecAI appear in ACC Build and vice versa.
webSheets.versionsDesc: Compare different revisions of the same drawing to identify changes. The comparison view highlights additions, deletions, and modifications.

webReports.overviewDesc: The Reports & Analytics module provides project dashboards, team performance metrics, and exportable reports to keep stakeholders informed.
webReports.dashboardDesc: The dashboard shows key project metrics at a glance including task completion rates, upcoming deadlines, team activity, and milestone status.
webReports.teamPerformanceDesc: Track team productivity with metrics like tasks completed, hours logged, and utilization rates. Identify bottlenecks and balance workloads across the team.
webReports.budgetDesc: Monitor project costs against budget. Track spending by team member, milestone, or task category. Set alerts for budget thresholds.
webReports.exportDesc: Export reports in multiple formats for sharing with stakeholders:
webReports.exportPdf: PDF: Formatted reports with charts and tables
webReports.exportExcel: Excel: Raw data for further analysis
webReports.exportCsv: CSV: Simple data export for integration

docsContent.getting-started:
Quick Start Guide

Welcome to InvoratecAI documentation. This guide will help you get started with our platform.

Prerequisites

  • Autodesk Revit 2019 or later
  • Windows 10/11
  • InvoratecAI Account

Installation Steps

  1. Download the InvoratecAI Add-in from your account dashboard
  2. Run the installer and follow the prompts
  3. Restart Revit to activate the add-in
  4. Sign in with your InvoratecAI credentials

Platform Overview

BIM AI (Revit Add-in)

Desktop application that integrates directly with Autodesk Revit, providing AI assistance, task management, and cloud synchronization.

Web AI (Cloud Platform)

Browser-based platform for project management, 3D model viewing, team collaboration, and reporting.

Key Features

🤖

AI-Powered Assistant

Use natural language to query your models and automate tasks

📋

Task Management

Create, assign, and track tasks with 45+ property types

🏗️

3D Model Viewer

View and analyze BIM models in your browser

☁️

ACC Integration

Seamless sync with Autodesk Construction Cloud
docsContent.installation:
Installation Guide

Follow these steps to install InvoratecAI on your system.

BIM AI (Revit Add-in)

System Requirements: Windows 10/11, Autodesk Revit 2019-2025, 8GB RAM minimum, .NET Framework 4.8

  1. Download the Installer

    Visit the Download page and select your Revit version

  2. Close Revit

    Ensure all Revit instances are closed before installation

  3. Run as Administrator

    Right-click the installer and select "Run as Administrator"

  4. Follow the Wizard

    Accept the license agreement and choose installation options

  5. Launch Revit

    Find InvoratecAI in the Add-ins tab on the Revit ribbon

Web AI (Cloud Platform)

No installation required! Access Web AI directly from your browser.

  1. Visit the Platform

    Go to app.invoratec.com

  2. Sign In or Register

    Use your existing account or create a new one

  3. Start Working

    Create a project or join an existing team

Supported Revit Versions

Revit VersionSupport Status
Revit 2025Full Support
Revit 2024Full Support
Revit 2023Full Support
Revit 2022Full Support
Revit 2021Full Support
Revit 2020Legacy Support
Revit 2019Legacy Support

Troubleshooting

Add-in not appearing in Revit

Ensure you ran the installer as Administrator and restart Revit completely.

Installation fails

Check that .NET Framework 4.8 is installed and you have write permissions to the Revit Add-ins folder.

License activation issues

Verify your internet connection and check that your subscription is active.
docsContent.configuration:
Configuration Guide

Learn how to configure InvoratecAI to match your workflow.

Account Settings

Manage your profile, subscription, and notification preferences in the account settings panel.

  • Profile: Update your name, email, and profile picture
  • Password: Change your password or enable two-factor authentication
  • Notifications: Configure email and in-app notification preferences
  • Language: Choose your preferred interface language
  • Timezone: Set your timezone for accurate scheduling

Project Settings

Configure project-specific settings including team members, milestones, and budget tracking.

General Settings

  • Project name and description
  • Project number and client information
  • Start and end dates
  • Project currency and units

Team Management

  • Invite team members by email
  • Assign roles (Admin, Editor, Viewer)
  • Set discipline and department
  • Configure hourly rates for budget tracking

Milestone Configuration

  • Create project milestones
  • Set target dates and budgets
  • Link deliverables to milestones
  • Track completion status

ACC Integration

Connect your Autodesk Construction Cloud account to sync projects, models, and coordination data.

  1. Navigate to Integrations

    Go to Settings > Integrations in your dashboard

  2. Click Connect ACC

    You'll be redirected to Autodesk's login page

  3. Authorize Access

    Grant InvoratecAI permission to access your ACC data

  4. Select Projects

    Choose which ACC projects to sync with InvoratecAI

Data Privacy: We only access the projects you explicitly select. Your credentials are never stored - we use secure OAuth 2.0 tokens.

BIM AI Settings

Configure the Revit add-in behavior and preferences.

  • Auto-sync: Enable automatic synchronization with the cloud
  • AI Model: Choose between standard and advanced AI processing
  • Voice Commands: Enable or disable voice control
  • Script Execution: Configure Python and C# script permissions
  • Tool Library Path: Set custom locations for tool storage
docsContent.web-tasks:
Overview

The Web Platform's task management system is built on a flexible database architecture supporting 45+ property types. Create unlimited custom pages with any combination of properties to match your project needs.

Creating Task Pages

Task pages are containers for organizing your work. Each page can have its own set of custom properties.

  1. Click the + button in the sidebar to create a new page
  2. Name your page and choose a template or start blank
  3. Add properties by clicking the + column header

Available Templates

  • RFI Tracker: Request for Information management
  • Submittal Log: Track submittals and approvals
  • Punch List: Deficiency tracking for closeout
  • Issue Log: General issue and conflict management
  • Change Orders: Track contract modifications

Property Types

InvoratecAI supports a wide variety of property types to capture any kind of project data:

Text Properties

  • Single-line text
  • Multi-line text (rich text)
  • URL links
  • Email addresses
  • Phone numbers

Number Properties

  • Integer numbers
  • Decimal numbers
  • Currency values
  • Percentage
  • Progress bar

Date & Time

  • Date picker
  • Date range
  • Time picker
  • Date/time combined
  • Created/Updated timestamps

Selection Properties

  • Single select (dropdown)
  • Multi-select (tags)
  • Status with colors
  • Priority levels
  • Checkbox

People & Relations

  • Person (assign team members)
  • Multi-person
  • Relation (link to other tasks)
  • Created by / Updated by

Media & Files

  • File attachments
  • Image gallery
  • Document links
  • Model element references

Smart Filtering

Filter your tasks by any property using the filter bar. Combine multiple filters with AND/OR logic. Save filter views for quick access to commonly used task lists.

Pro Tip: Create saved views like "My Open Tasks", "Overdue Items", or "High Priority" for quick access.

View Options

Switch between different views to visualize your data:

  • Table View: Spreadsheet-like grid for detailed editing
  • Kanban Board: Drag-and-drop cards by status
  • Calendar View: See tasks by date
  • Timeline View: Gantt chart visualization
  • Gallery View: Card-based view with images

Excel Import/Export

Bulk import tasks from Excel spreadsheets or export your data for reporting:

  1. Click the Import button and select your Excel file
  2. Map columns to properties in the import dialog
  3. Review and confirm the import
docsContent.web-viewer:
Overview

The 3D Model Viewer is powered by Autodesk Platform Services (APS) and supports viewing IFC, NWD, RVT, and other BIM formats directly in your browser without installing any software.

Supported File Formats

FormatDescriptionSupport
.rvtAutodesk RevitFull
.ifcIndustry Foundation ClassesFull
.nwd/.nwcNavisworksFull
.dwgAutoCADFull
.dwfxDesign Web FormatFull

Navigation Controls

Use these controls to navigate your 3D model:

Mouse Controls

  • Left-click + drag: Orbit around the model
  • Right-click + drag: Pan the view
  • Scroll wheel: Zoom in/out
  • Double-click: Fit element to view
  • Ctrl + click: Multi-select elements

Keyboard Shortcuts

  • F: Fit all to view
  • H: Hide selected elements
  • I: Isolate selected elements
  • S: Show all elements
  • Esc: Clear selection

Element Selection

Click on any element to select it. The property panel will show all parameters and properties for the selected element. Multi-select using Ctrl+click.

Element Isolation

Right-click on selected elements and choose 'Isolate' to hide all other elements. This helps you focus on specific areas of your model. Use 'Show All' to restore visibility.

Section Planes

Create section cuts through your model using the section plane tools. Adjust the position and orientation of section planes to expose interior details.

  1. Click the Section tool in the toolbar
  2. Choose X, Y, or Z plane orientation
  3. Drag the slider to adjust the cut position
  4. Use multiple planes for box sections

Measurement Tools

Measure distances, areas, and angles directly in the 3D view.

  • Distance: Click two points to measure length
  • Area: Click multiple points to measure enclosed area
  • Angle: Click three points to measure angles
  • Calibrate: Set a known distance for accurate scaling

Property Inspector

The property panel displays all BIM properties for selected elements including:

  • Type parameters (shared across instances)
  • Instance parameters (unique to each element)
  • Calculated values (area, volume, length)
  • Custom properties from the source file
  • Material information
docsContent.web-timeline:
Overview

The Timeline & Gantt view provides visual project scheduling with task dependencies, milestones, and progress tracking. Plan your project timeline and monitor progress in real-time.

Gantt Chart View

The Gantt chart displays tasks as horizontal bars on a timeline. The length of each bar represents the task duration. Tasks are color-coded by status.

Not Started
In Progress
Completed
Overdue

Scheduling Tasks

Schedule tasks using these methods:

  1. Drag horizontally: Move task bars to change start dates
  2. Drag edges: Extend or shorten the task duration
  3. Properties panel: Set exact dates in the task details
  4. Quick add: Click on the timeline to add tasks at specific dates

Task Dependencies

Link tasks together to create dependencies. When a predecessor task moves, dependent tasks automatically adjust.

Dependency Types

  • Finish-to-Start (FS): Task B starts when Task A finishes
  • Start-to-Start (SS): Task B starts when Task A starts
  • Finish-to-Finish (FF): Task B finishes when Task A finishes
  • Start-to-Finish (SF): Task B finishes when Task A starts

Use the Relation property to link tasks together, or drag connection handles between task bars.

Milestones

Mark important project dates as milestones. Milestones appear as diamond shapes on the timeline and can be linked to tasks and deliverables.

  • Create milestones from the toolbar or convert existing tasks
  • Set target dates for key deliverables
  • Track milestone completion status
  • Receive notifications for approaching milestones

Progress Tracking

Track completion percentage for each task. The Gantt chart shows progress as a filled portion of the task bar.

Pro Tip: Use the "% Complete" column to quickly update progress across multiple tasks.

Timeline Scale

Adjust the timeline scale to view your project at different granularities:

  • Day: Detailed view for short-term planning
  • Week: Standard view for most projects
  • Month: Overview for long-term projects
  • Quarter: Strategic planning view
docsContent.web-coordination:
Overview

The Coordination module helps you manage multi-discipline clash detection and resolution. Sync clashes from Autodesk Construction Cloud and track resolution status.

ACC Sync

Connect to your ACC project to import coordination clashes:

  1. Navigate to Coordination

    Open the Coordination page from your project dashboard

  2. Click 'Sync from ACC'

    Select your ACC project and clash test

  3. Import Clashes

    Clashes are imported with their 3D viewpoints and metadata

Auto-Sync: Enable automatic synchronization to keep clashes up-to-date as new tests are run in ACC.

Reviewing Clashes

Each clash includes a 3D viewpoint that zooms to the conflict location. Review clashes in context with the full model visible.

Clash Details

  • Element A and Element B identification
  • Clash distance and type (hard/soft)
  • Associated viewpoint
  • Source file information

Review Actions

  • Approve - Mark as acceptable
  • Reject - Requires design change
  • Defer - Review later
  • Assign - Send to team member

Issue Tracking

Assign clashes to team members, set priority and due dates, and track resolution status.

  • Assignment: Assign to responsible party by discipline
  • Priority: Set High, Medium, or Low priority
  • Due Date: Set resolution deadline
  • Comments: Add discussion and notes
  • Attachments: Upload resolution documents

Discipline Tags

Tag clashes by discipline (MEP, Structural, Architectural) for filtering and reporting.

Mechanical
Electrical
Plumbing
Structural
Architectural
Fire Protection

Reports

Generate reports showing clash counts by discipline, status, and resolution progress.

  • Summary dashboard with key metrics
  • Export to PDF for stakeholder meetings
  • Trend analysis over time
  • Team performance metrics
docsContent.web-sheets:
Overview

The Shop Drawings module provides 2D drawing viewing with markup tools and ACC Build integration. Review, annotate, and approve construction documents.

Viewing Drawings

Upload PDF or DWG files to view in the browser. Navigate between pages, zoom, pan, and search for text within documents.

Navigation

  • Thumbnail panel for quick page navigation
  • Page number input for direct access
  • Previous/Next buttons
  • Zoom controls (+/-/fit)

Search

  • Full-text search within PDFs
  • Highlight search results
  • Navigate between matches

Markup Tools

Annotate drawings with these markup tools:

Drawing Tools

  • Pen (freehand drawing)
  • Highlighter
  • Rectangle, Circle, Line
  • Arrow and Callout
  • Cloud markup

Annotation Tools

  • Text annotations
  • Stamps (Approved, Rejected, etc.)
  • Checkmarks and X marks
  • Dimension measurements
  • Links to tasks

ACC Build Integration

Sync markups with ACC Build to share annotations with the field team. Markups created in InvoratecAI appear in ACC Build and vice versa.

  1. Connect your ACC project in Settings
  2. Markups sync automatically in both directions
  3. View field team annotations in real-time
  4. Collaborate across office and site

Version Comparison

Compare different revisions of the same drawing to identify changes.

  • Side-by-side: View two versions next to each other
  • Overlay: Superimpose versions with transparency
  • Difference: Highlight additions and deletions
  • Swipe: Drag slider to reveal changes

Approval Workflow

Manage the review and approval process for shop drawings:

  1. Upload drawing for review
  2. Assign reviewers by discipline
  3. Reviewers add markups and comments
  4. Mark as Approved, Approved as Noted, or Rejected
  5. Track revision history
docsContent.web-reports:
Overview

The Reports & Analytics module provides project dashboards, team performance metrics, and exportable reports to keep stakeholders informed.

Project Dashboard

The dashboard shows key project metrics at a glance:

Task Metrics

  • Total tasks and completion rate
  • Tasks by status breakdown
  • Overdue items count
  • Tasks due this week

Project Health

  • Schedule variance
  • Budget utilization
  • Milestone status
  • Risk indicators

Team Performance

Track team productivity with metrics like tasks completed, hours logged, and utilization rates.

  • Tasks Completed: Track completion by team member
  • Hours Logged: Time tracking summaries
  • Utilization Rate: Logged hours vs. available capacity
  • Workload Balance: Identify overloaded team members
  • Activity Feed: Recent actions and updates

Pro Tip: Use the team leaderboard to recognize top performers and motivate the team.

Budget Tracking

Monitor project costs against budget. Track spending by team member, milestone, or task category.

  • Total budget vs. actual spend
  • Cost breakdown by milestone
  • Burn rate analysis
  • Forecast remaining budget
  • Alert thresholds (80%, 90%, 100%)

Exporting Reports

Export reports in multiple formats for sharing with stakeholders:

📄

PDF Export

Formatted reports with charts, tables, and branding

📊

Excel Export

Raw data for further analysis in spreadsheets

📋

CSV Export

Simple data format for integration with other tools

Scheduled Reports

Set up automatic report delivery to stakeholders:

  1. Choose report type and filters
  2. Select delivery frequency (daily, weekly, monthly)
  3. Add recipient email addresses
  4. Reports are delivered automatically as PDF attachments
docsContent.revit-assistant:
Overview

The AI Assistant lets you control Revit using natural language. Describe what you want to do in plain English, and the AI will execute the appropriate commands or scripts.

Privacy Note: Your model data is processed securely and is NEVER used to train AI models.

Natural Language Commands

Type or speak commands in conversational language. The AI understands context and can handle complex requests.

Example Commands

  • Selection: "Select all walls on Level 1"
  • Modification: "Change the height of selected doors to 2100mm"
  • Analysis: "Find all elements with missing fire rating parameter"
  • Creation: "Create a schedule of all mechanical equipment"
  • Export: "Export a list of all room areas to Excel"
  • Query: "How many windows are in this model?"

Voice Control

Click the microphone button to speak commands hands-free. Voice control is perfect for when you're working in the model and don't want to switch to the keyboard.

  • Press and hold the mic button to record
  • Release to process your command
  • Works in noisy environments with noise cancellation
  • Supports multiple languages

Code Execution

The AI can write and execute Python or C# scripts to accomplish complex tasks.

Safety: Review generated code before execution. The AI shows you the code and asks for confirmation before running.

  • Python scripts using Revit API
  • C# scripts for complex operations
  • Save useful scripts to your Tool Library
  • Share scripts with your team

Smart Tool Matching

The AI automatically matches your request to available tools in your library. If a suitable tool exists, it will suggest using it instead of writing new code.

  1. You describe what you want to do
  2. AI searches your Tool Library for matching tools
  3. If found, suggests the existing tool
  4. If not, offers to create a new script

Context Awareness

The AI understands the context of your current session:

  • Current selection: Works with your selected elements
  • Active view: Knows which view you're in
  • Previous commands: Maintains conversation context
  • Model structure: Understands your project's organization
docsContent.revit-tasks:
Overview

Manage tasks directly within Revit without switching to a browser. Tasks sync with the Web Platform so your team always has the latest information.

Element Tracking

Link tasks to specific Revit elements for easy navigation:

  1. Select elements in the Revit view
  2. Click 'Link to Task' in the task panel
  3. Click the element link to zoom to that element anytime

Pro Tip: Linked elements are highlighted when viewing the task, making it easy to find issues in complex models.

Creating Tasks

Create tasks directly from Revit:

  • Quick Add: Use the + button in the Tasks panel
  • From Selection: Right-click elements and choose "Create Task"
  • From Clash: Convert coordination clashes to tasks
  • Voice Command: Say "Create a task for..."

Time Tracking

Track time spent on tasks with the built-in timer.

  • Start timer: Click the clock icon when you begin work
  • Auto-pause: Timer pauses when Revit loses focus
  • Manual entry: Add time entries for offline work
  • Reports: View time summaries by task or project

Cloud Sync

All changes sync automatically with the cloud. Team members on the Web Platform see your updates in real-time, and you see theirs.

Green indicator: Synced and up-to-date
Yellow indicator: Syncing in progress
Red indicator: Offline or sync error

My Tasks View

The My Tasks view shows only tasks assigned to you.

  • Filter by status (To Do, In Progress, Done)
  • Filter by priority (High, Medium, Low)
  • Filter by due date (Overdue, Due Today, Due This Week)
  • Sort by any property

Task Panel Features

Task Details

  • Title and description
  • Assignee and due date
  • Priority and status
  • Linked elements
  • Comments thread

Quick Actions

  • Mark complete
  • Change status
  • Reassign
  • Add comment
  • Zoom to element
docsContent.revit-tools:
Overview

The Tool Library manages Python scripts, C# scripts, and compiled DLL plugins. Run automation tools from a single interface and share tools with your team.

DLL Plugins

Load compiled Revit plugins (.dll files) into the tool library. A single DLL can contain multiple commands, each appearing as a separate tool.

  • Tools are loaded automatically at Revit startup
  • Support for external commands and applications
  • Version tracking and updates
  • Dependencies managed automatically

Python Scripts

Execute Python scripts using the IronPython engine. Scripts have full access to the Revit API and can automate any task.

# Example: Select all walls on current level
from Autodesk.Revit.DB import *

doc = __revit__.ActiveUIDocument.Document
view = doc.ActiveView
level = view.GenLevel

walls = FilteredElementCollector(doc)\
    .OfClass(Wall)\
    .WhereElementIsNotElementType()\
    .ToElements()

level_walls = [w for w in walls if w.LevelId == level.Id]
print(f"Found {'}len(level_walls)} walls on {'}level.Name}")

C# Scripts

Run C# code on-the-fly without compilation. The C# scripting engine compiles and executes your code instantly.

// Example: Rename all views by adding prefix
using Autodesk.Revit.DB;

var views = new FilteredElementCollector(doc)
    .OfClass(typeof(View))
    .Cast<View>()
    .Where(v => !v.IsTemplate);

using (Transaction t = new Transaction(doc, "Rename Views"))
{'}
    t.Start();
    foreach (var view in views)
    {'}
        view.Name = "PRJ-" + view.Name;
    }
    t.Commit();
}

Organizing Tools

Organize tools into categories for easy discovery.

  • Categories: Group tools by function (Selection, Export, Analysis, etc.)
  • Tags: Add keywords for searchability
  • Descriptions: Document what each tool does
  • Icons: Custom icons for quick identification
  • Favorites: Pin frequently used tools

Sharing Tools

Share tools with your team through the cloud:

  1. Right-click a tool and select "Share"
  2. Choose team members or share with entire organization
  3. Shared tools sync automatically
  4. Updates push to all users

Team Libraries: Create shared tool libraries that sync across your organization.

AI Tool Generation

Use the AI Assistant to generate new tools:

  1. Describe what you want the tool to do
  2. AI generates Python or C# code
  3. Review and test the generated script
  4. Save to your Tool Library for reuse

docsContent.revit-acc:
Overview

The ACC Integration connects InvoratecAI to your Autodesk Construction Cloud projects. Sync models, import clashes, and manage project files from within Revit.

Connecting to ACC

Connect your Autodesk account using secure OAuth authentication:

  1. Click 'Connect to ACC' in the ACC Hub panel
  2. Sign in with your Autodesk ID in the browser
  3. Authorize InvoratecAI to access your ACC projects
  4. Select the ACC project to link to your InvoratecAI project

Security: We use OAuth 2.0 and never store your Autodesk password. You can revoke access anytime from your Autodesk account settings.

Hub Browser

Browse your ACC hubs and projects in a familiar tree structure. Navigate folders and view files without leaving Revit.

  • View all hubs and projects you have access to
  • Navigate folder structure
  • Preview files before opening
  • Download files to local storage
  • Upload files directly from Revit

Model Sync

Publish Revit models to your ACC project with one click.

  1. Click Publish to ACC in the toolbar
  2. Select destination folder in ACC
  3. Choose version name and notes
  4. Model uploads and translates automatically
  5. View in Web Platform's 3D viewer

Clash Import

Import coordination clashes from ACC Model Coordination.

  • Select clash test from ACC
  • Clashes import with 3D viewpoints
  • Assign to team members
  • Track resolution status
  • Sync status back to ACC

Two-way Sync: Resolution status syncs back to ACC, keeping both platforms in alignment.

Document Management

Access and manage ACC documents from Revit:

docsContent.api-reference:
API Reference

InvoratecAI provides a REST API for integration with your existing tools and workflows.

Authentication

All API requests require authentication using an API key. Generate your API key in the account settings.

// Request Header Example
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
Content-Type: application/json

Base URL

https://api.invoratec.com/v1

Available Endpoints

Our API includes endpoints for projects, tasks, users, and AI queries.

MethodEndpointDescription
GET/projectsList all projects
GET/projects/:idGet project details
POST/projectsCreate new project
GET/projects/:id/tasksList tasks in project
POST/projects/:id/tasksCreate new task
PATCH/tasks/:idUpdate task
DELETE/tasks/:idDelete task
POST/ai/querySend AI query

Rate Limits

API requests are limited based on your subscription plan:

PlanRequests/MinRequests/Day
Starter6010,000
Professional12050,000
EnterpriseCustomCustom

Example Request

// Create a new task
fetch('https://api.invoratec.com/v1/projects/abc123/tasks', {'}
  method: 'POST',
  headers: {'}
    'Authorization': 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({'}
    title: 'Review mechanical drawings',
    assignee: 'user@example.com',
    priority: 'high',
    dueDate: '2025-01-15'
  })
})

Error Handling

The API returns standard HTTP status codes:

  • 200: Success
  • 201: Created
  • 400: Bad Request - Invalid parameters
  • 401: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key
  • 403: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions
  • 404: Not Found - Resource doesn't exist
  • 429: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded
  • 500: Server Error - Contact support

legalContent.security:
At InvoratecAI, we take the security of your data seriously. This Security Policy outlines our comprehensive protection measures and our commitment to maintaining the highest security standards.

1. Infrastructure Security (Shared Responsibility)

Understanding the Shared Responsibility Model

InvoratecAI is built on Google Cloud Platform (GCP), which maintains SOC 1, SOC 2, SOC 3, ISO 27001, and other certifications. However, it's important to understand how cloud security works:

  • GCP's Responsibility: Physical security, hardware, network infrastructure, and their managed services
  • InvoratecAI's Responsibility: Application security, data handling, access controls, encryption implementation, and secure coding practices

While we benefit from GCP's certified infrastructure, InvoratecAI implements our own comprehensive security controls at the application layer to ensure your data is protected end-to-end.

  • Cloud Platform: Google Cloud Platform enterprise-grade infrastructure
  • Data Centers: GCP's world-class physical security with 24/7 monitoring
  • Redundancy: Multi-region deployment for high availability

2. Data Protection

  • Encryption at Rest: AES-256 encryption for all stored data
  • Encryption in Transit: TLS 1.3 for all data transmission
  • Isolated Storage: Data segregated by organization with strict access controls
  • Backup & Recovery: Regular automated backups with tested recovery procedures
  • Data Retention: Clear policies with secure deletion when data is no longer needed

3. Application Security

  • Secure Development: Security-focused development practices and code reviews
  • Authentication: Industry-standard authentication with support for SSO
  • Authorization: Role-based access control (RBAC) for granular permissions
  • Input Validation: Comprehensive input validation to prevent injection attacks
  • Dependency Management: Regular updates and vulnerability scanning of dependencies

4. AI Security

Your Data Privacy is Protected

  • Your data is NOT used to train AI models
  • AI queries are processed ephemerally and not stored after processing
  • Your BIM models and project data remain confidential
  • AI Provider: Anthropic Claude with enterprise security standards
  • Data Isolation: Each query is processed in isolation
  • No Training: Customer data never used for model training
  • Ephemeral Processing: Query data deleted immediately after response

5. Network Security

  • DDoS Protection: Built-in protection against distributed denial-of-service attacks
  • Firewall: Web Application Firewall (WAF) to protect against common web exploits
  • Monitoring: 24/7 network monitoring and anomaly detection
  • Access Control: IP allowlisting and VPN support for enterprise customers

6. Operational Security

  • Access Management: Strict employee access controls with principle of least privilege
  • Logging & Monitoring: Comprehensive audit logging of all system access
  • Incident Response: Documented incident response procedures with defined escalation paths
  • Vendor Management: Security assessment of all third-party vendors and sub-processors

7. Compliance

  • GDPR: Compliant with European data protection regulations
  • Infrastructure Certifications: Built on GCP's SOC 2, ISO 27001 certified infrastructure
  • Regular Assessments: Ongoing security assessments and improvements
  • Sub-processor Oversight: All sub-processors vetted for security compliance

8. Security Contact

We are committed to maintaining the security of your data. If you have security concerns or discover a vulnerability, please contact us:

Email: contact@invoratec.com

Report Vulnerabilities: We welcome responsible disclosure of security vulnerabilities.

  • Session Storage: Temporary storage cleared when browser closes

This Data Processing Agreement ("DPA") forms part of the Terms of Service between InvoratecAI ("Processor") and the Customer ("Controller") and governs the processing of personal data in connection with InvoratecAI's BIM AI and Web AI services.

1. Definitions

  • Controller: The customer organization that determines the purposes and means of processing personal data
  • Processor: InvoratecAI, which processes personal data on behalf of the Controller
  • Sub-processor: Third parties engaged by InvoratecAI to process personal data
  • Personal Data: Any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person
  • Processing: Any operation performed on personal data (collection, storage, use, disclosure, deletion)
  • Data Subject: An individual whose personal data is processed
  • Applicable Data Protection Laws: GDPR, CCPA, and other relevant privacy regulations

2. Scope of Processing

Subject Matter

Processing of personal data in connection with providing BIM AI and Web AI services.

Types of Personal Data

  • Account information (names, email addresses, job titles)
  • Usage data and analytics
  • Project collaboration data (comments, task assignments)
  • Authentication and access logs

Categories of Data Subjects

  • Customer employees and contractors
  • Project collaborators and stakeholders

3. Processor Obligations

InvoratecAI shall:

  • Process personal data only on documented instructions from the Controller
  • Ensure personnel authorized to process data are bound by confidentiality obligations
  • Implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures
  • Engage sub-processors only with prior authorization and equivalent contractual obligations
  • Assist the Controller in responding to data subject requests
  • Assist with data protection impact assessments and regulatory consultations
  • Delete or return all personal data upon termination of services
  • Provide information necessary to demonstrate compliance with this DPA

4. Controller Obligations

The Controller shall:

  • Ensure lawful basis for processing personal data
  • Provide necessary instructions for data processing
  • Ensure data subjects are informed about the processing
  • Comply with applicable data protection laws

5. Security Measures

InvoratecAI implements comprehensive security measures including:

  • Encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
  • Access Control: Role-based access with MFA support
  • Infrastructure: Google Cloud Platform with enterprise security
  • Monitoring: 24/7 security monitoring and incident response
  • Data Isolation: Organization-level data segregation
  • Backup: Regular automated backups with tested recovery

See our Security Policy for full details.

6. Sub-processors

The Controller authorizes InvoratecAI to engage sub-processors listed on our Sub-processors page.

InvoratecAI will:

  • Notify the Controller of any intended changes to sub-processors
  • Ensure sub-processors are bound by equivalent data protection obligations
  • Remain liable for the acts and omissions of sub-processors

7. Data Subject Rights

InvoratecAI will assist the Controller in fulfilling data subject requests for:

  • Access to personal data
  • Rectification of inaccurate data
  • Erasure (right to be forgotten)
  • Data portability
  • Restriction of processing
  • Objection to processing

8. Data Breach Notification

In the event of a personal data breach, InvoratecAI will:

  • Notify the Controller without undue delay (within 72 hours where feasible)
  • Provide details of the breach and affected data
  • Document the breach and remediation steps taken
  • Cooperate with the Controller's response and notification obligations

9. International Data Transfers

Personal data may be transferred to countries outside the EEA. InvoratecAI ensures adequate protection through:

  • Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission
  • Data processing agreements with sub-processors
  • Assessment of destination country's data protection laws

10. Audit Rights

The Controller may request information or conduct audits to verify compliance with this DPA. InvoratecAI will:

  • Provide relevant documentation and evidence upon request
  • Allow on-site audits with reasonable notice (costs borne by Controller)
  • Accept audit reports from qualified third-party auditors

11. Duration and Termination

This DPA remains in effect for the duration of the service agreement. Upon termination:

  • InvoratecAI will delete or return all personal data within 30 days
  • Backups will be purged within 90 days
  • Controller may request certification of data deletion

12. Contact

For DPA-related inquiries:

Email: contact@invoratec.com

Transparency: You can see exactly what permissions are requested before connecting any integration.

4. Data Handling

  • Data Flow: Data flows directly between your integrated services and InvoratecAI
  • Caching: We cache data locally for performance; you can clear cache anytime
  • Synchronization: Choose one-way or two-way sync as needed
  • Privacy: Your integration data is subject to our Privacy Policy

5. Connecting Integrations

How to Connect

  1. Go to Settings > Integrations in your InvoratecAI dashboard
  2. Click "Connect" on the integration you want to add
  3. Sign in to your third-party account when prompted
  4. Authorize the requested permissions
  5. Select which projects to sync (if applicable)

Managing Connections

  • View all connected integrations in Settings
  • Disconnect integrations at any time
  • Refresh tokens if connection expires
  • View sync status and last sync time

6. Troubleshooting

Common issues and solutions:

  • Connection Expired: Re-authenticate by clicking "Reconnect" in Settings
  • Missing Projects: Ensure you have access to the project in the source platform
  • Sync Errors: Check if you have write permissions for two-way sync
  • Slow Performance: Large projects may take longer; use selective sync

7. Requesting New Integrations

We're always expanding our integration ecosystem. If you need an integration we don't currently support, let us know:

  • Submit integration requests through our feedback form
  • Enterprise customers can request custom integrations
  • We prioritize based on customer demand

8. Contact

For integration support or questions:

Email: contact@invoratec.com
